Client.title: Client
Client.description: Represents a customer of the barber shop.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Client entity.

name.description: The full name of the client.

notes.description: Any specific notes or details about the client, such as hair type or preferences.

preferences.description: Specific preferences or requests of the client for their services.

createdAt.description: The date and time when the client record was created.

updatedAt.description: The date and time when the client record was last updated.

Service.title: Service
Service.description: Defines a service offered by the barber shop, including its details and pricing.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Service entity.

name.description: The name of the service offered (e.g., 'Corte Masculino', 'Barba', 'Química').

description.description: A brief description of the service.

durationMinutes.description: The estimated duration of the service in minutes.

isActive.description: Indicates if the service is currently offered and available for booking.

createdAt.description: The date and time when the service record was created.

updatedAt.description: The date and time when the service record was last updated.

Barber.title: Barber
Barber.description: Represents a barber using the application to manage their schedule and services. (Assumed to be the main user of the app).

id.description: Unique identifier for the Barber entity.

name.description: The full name of the barber.

description.description: A short biography or description of the barber, potentially for client viewing.

createdAt.description: The date and time when the barber record was created.

updatedAt.description: The date and time when the barber record was last updated.

Appointment.title: Appointment
Appointment.description: Records a scheduled service booking for a client with a barber.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Appointment entity.

clientId.description: Reference to the client who booked the appointment. (Relationship: Client 1:N Appointment)

barberId.description: Reference to the barber assigned to the appointment. (Relationship: Barber 1:N Appointment)

serviceId.description: Reference to the service booked for the appointment. (Relationship: Service 1:N Appointment)

startTime.description: The date and time when the appointment is scheduled to start.

endTime.description: The date and time when the appointment is scheduled to end.

status.description: The current status of the appointment (e.g., 'scheduled', 'completed', 'cancelled', 'no-show').

priceAtTimeOfBooking.description: The price of the service at the time the appointment was booked, to preserve historical financial data in case service prices change.

notes.description: Any specific notes or instructions for this particular appointment.

createdAt.description: The date and time when the appointment record was created.

updatedAt.description: The date and time when the appointment record was last updated.

BlockedTime.title: BlockedTime
BlockedTime.description: Represents periods when a barber is unavailable for appointments, such as breaks, personal time, or days off.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BlockedTime entity.

barberId.description: Reference to the barber whose time is being blocked. (Relationship: Barber 1:N BlockedTime)

startTime.description: The date and time when the blocked period starts.

endTime.description: The date and time when the blocked period ends.

reason.description: A description of why the time is blocked (e.g., 'Lunch Break', 'Day Off', 'Meeting', 'Holiday').

createdAt.description: The date and time when the blocked time record was created.

updatedAt.description: The date and time when the blocked time record was last updated.

definition.description: Top-level collection for storing individual barber profiles. The document ID (`barberId`) MUST be the Firebase Authentication UID of the barber. This is the root for all barber-specific data.

params.name: barberId
params.description: The unique identifier of the barber, corresponding to Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Subcollection for clients managed by a specific barber. Each client document belongs to the barber identified by the parent `barberId`. Access is restricted to the owning barber. Includes implicit ownership via path structure.

params.name: barberId
params.description: The unique identifier of the barber, corresponding to Firebase Authentication UID.

params.name: clientId
params.description: The unique identifier for the client.

definition.description: Subcollection for services offered by a specific barber. Each service document belongs to the barber identified by the parent `barberId`. Access is restricted to the owning barber. Includes implicit ownership via path structure.

params.name: barberId
params.description: The unique identifier of the barber, corresponding to Firebase Authentication UID.

params.name: serviceId
params.description: The unique identifier for the service.

definition.description: Subcollection for appointments scheduled with a specific barber. Each appointment document belongs to the barber identified by the parent `barberId`. Document MUST contain a `barberId` field that matches the path's `barberId` for authorization independence. Access is restricted to the owning barber.

params.name: barberId
params.description: The unique identifier of the barber, corresponding to Firebase Authentication UID.

params.name: appointmentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the appointment.

definition.description: Subcollection for blocked time slots for a specific barber. Each blocked time document belongs to the barber identified by the parent `barberId`. Document MUST contain a `barberId` field that matches the path's `barberId` for authorization independence. Access is restricted to the owning barber.

params.name: barberId
params.description: The unique identifier of the barber, corresponding to Firebase Authentication UID.

params.name: blockedTimeId
params.description: The unique identifier for the blocked time slot.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed with a strong emphasis on Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and Path-Based Access Modeling to ensure robust security rules and efficient query patterns. The core principle is that all data related to a specific barber is nested under their unique user ID, which directly corresponds to `request.auth.uid` from Firebase Authentication.

1.  **Authorization Independence:** All subcollections (`clients`, `services`, `appointments`, `blockedTimes`) are directly owned by and nested under a specific `/barbers/{barberId}` document. This means that authorization for any document in these subcollections can be determined by checking `request.auth.uid == barberId` (where `barberId` is the path segment). For `Appointment` and `BlockedTime` documents, the `barberId` field within the document is explicitly denormalized to match the parent path's `barberId`. This redundancy is critical: it eliminates the need for expensive and complex `get()` calls in security rules to check parent document ownership, allowing for atomic operations and simplified rule logic (e.g., `allow write: if request.auth.uid == resource.data.barberId && request.auth.uid == barberId;`).

2.  **Structural Segregation:** Each collection and subcollection (`barbers`, `clients`, `services`, `appointments`, `blockedTimes`) maintains a homogeneous security posture. For example, all `Client` documents under a given `/barbers/{barberId}/clients` path are accessible *only* by that specific barber. There are no mixed-access scenarios within a single collection, which greatly simplifies security rules. Public data, if any, would reside in separate top-level collections.

3.  **QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** This structure inherently supports Query-As-Permissions (QAPs). Since all client, service, appointment, and blocked time data is nested under the `/barbers/{barberId}` path, listing operations are naturally secure and efficient. A barber can only query for their own clients (`/barbers/{barberId}/clients`), services, appointments, or blocked times. The Firestore security rules can simply allow reads on these paths if `request.auth.uid` matches the `{barberId}` wildcard, ensuring that queries are always implicitly filtered by the user's authentication context. This design prevents unintended data exposure and eliminates the need for complex, performance-intensive security rule filtering.

4.  **DBAC (Database-Backed Access Control):** Barber profiles are stored in the `/barbers/{barberId}` collection, where `barberId` matches the Firebase Authentication `uid`. This provides a clear, database-backed record of each barber. Any future roles (e.g., shop managers, administrators) could be managed in dedicated role collections (e.g., `/roles_admin/{uid}`) following the "Existence over Content" principle, further enhancing DBAC without using custom claims.
